commands.guard: HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 python3 -m compileall -q hipengine tests scripts && HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 pytest -q tests/test_generation_batch_scheduler.py tests/test_generation_qwen35_paro.py tests/test_qwen35_resident_batch_layout.py tests/test_kvcache_policy.py tests/test_kvcache_spans.py tests/test_server_api.py -q && HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 python3 scripts/qwen35_batch_correctness.py --rows 2 --json /tmp/hipengine-e2e-primitive-c2.json && HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 python3 scripts/qwen35_batch_correctness.py --rows 8 --json /tmp/hipengine-e2e-primitive-c8.json
commands.profiler_smoke: HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 rocprofv3 --kernel-trace --output-format csv -d /tmp/hipengine-iter428-c8-profiler/raw -o c8-current -- python3 scripts/qwen35_batch_retained_bench.py --model /models/hipengine/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-PARO-full4096-e5-packed-MTP-BF16 --fixture /tmp/hipengine-prebench/fixtures/qwen36_paro_8x512_prompt_ids.json --prompt-length 512 --batch-size 8 --decode-tokens 128 --warmup-decode-tokens 8 --max-layers 40 --compiler-version-file /tmp/hipengine-retained/hipcc-version.txt --require-cached-build --json /tmp/hipengine-iter428-c8-profiler/c8-profiler-bench.json

conclusion: C8 profiler evidence shows projection kernels dominate the filtered trace (~46.7%), followed by fused norm/rotary/other (~33.1%), attention (~14.7%), MoE (~5.5%), and sampling (<0.1%). C8 has correctness + positive scaling + profiler evidence, but no retained performance claim is allowed while row-chunk full-attention and exact command-shape blockers remain.

profiler.notes: Current-commit c8 512/128 rocprof smoke. Filtered trace removes ROCclr transfer/fill noise and single-row argmax validation rows; no retained performance claim is made.
